bead.title: server: harden document path confinement end-to-end (REST + GraphQL read/write) against symlink escape
bead.description:
PROBLEM: Document read/write paths use a fragile strings.Contains(cleaned,"..") check that does not catch symlink escapes out of the library root. Both the codex and opus reviews flagged this as the single most important fix (only item that can read/write outside the intended root).
ROOT CAUSE: server.go:1357-1361 (handleReadDocument), :1396-1400 (handleWriteDocument), :4197-4198 (handleDocWrite); GraphQL write resolver_mutation_docs.go:45; AND GraphQL read fallback resolver_documents.go:67-68 (join+read without strong confinement). A symlink inside libPath pointing outside is not caught. A hardened helper already exists for run-bundle download: state_runs_detail.go:111-140 (filepath.Rel + EvalSymlinks).
PROPOSED FIX: replace all five ad-hoc checks with the state_runs_detail.go confinement helper (Rel + EvalSymlinks rooted at the library/project root). 
NON-SCOPE: search, beads, executions paths.
bead.acceptance:
1. handleReadDocument/handleWriteDocument/handleDocWrite, resolver_mutation_docs.go write, and resolver_documents.go read-fallback all route through one Rel+EvalSymlinks confinement helper rooted at the library root.
2. TestDocumentPathConfinement_RejectsSymlinkEscape (cli/internal/server) creates a symlink inside the root pointing outside and asserts read AND write are rejected for REST and GraphQL paths.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes
